headline: Is Labor Tensions And Softer Cash Flows Altering The Investment Case For Sysco (SYY)?
summary: In recent days, drivers at Sysco Western Minnesota, represented by Teamsters Local 120, unanimously authorized a strike if a new collective bargaining agreement is not reached after their existing contract expired on May 17, adding labor uncertainty to the foodservice distributor’s operations. At the same time, commentary highlighting weak sales volumes, pressure on return on invested capital, and a softer cash flow outlook has amplified concerns that Sysco faces rising operational risks...

headline: Don’t Look Now, But 4 Blue-Chip Giants Could Be the Newest Dividend Kings
summary: Companies that have raised dividends for shareholders for 50 years or more are the kinds of investments passive income investors need to own. Dependability is crucial for individuals seeking to increase their annual income through dividend stock investments. The Dividend Kings are the 57 companies that have raised their dividends for at least 50 years, ... Don’t Look Now, But 4 Blue-Chip Giants Could Be the Newest Dividend Kings
